nodes.label: AI as collaborative co-creator for tailored educational materials
nodes.label_negative: AI as autonomous generator requiring minimal input
nodes.grounding_span: I collaborate heavily. I often have to give multiple prompts in order to get AI to meet the requirements I want.

nodes.label: AI as time-saving tool for routine tasks
nodes.grounding_span: This is a task that used to take me ages to complete, so saving all this time is very satisfying.

nodes.label: AI reliability and honesty in task execution
nodes.label_negative: AI unreliability and tendency to make mistakes
nodes.grounding_span: I look forward to it being more honest, and less likely to make mistakes

nodes.label: AI as research filter for educational literature
nodes.label_negative: Direct reading of all educational research
nodes.grounding_span: I also use AI for summarizing educational research for me

nodes.label: AI as sounding board for workplace relationships
nodes.label_negative: Handling workplace dynamics without external input
nodes.grounding_span: I sometimes use AI to help me understand and work on my relationships with my colleagues.

nodes.label: Student-centered education and accessibility
nodes.grounding_span: create a really simplified text that aligns well with textbooks but is written in a simple manner that will work well for my ESL students

nodes.label: Professional efficiency and time savings
nodes.grounding_span: saving all this time is very satisfying

nodes.label: Epistemic understanding and staying current
nodes.grounding_span: I like to keep in touch with the latest research on how people learn

nodes.label: Personal well-being and workplace harmony
nodes.grounding_span: gives me a bit of 'therapy' and helps me manage my day

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for creating accessible materials
nodes.grounding_span: I usually like enjoy when I create a really simplified text that aligns well with textbooks but is written in a simple manner that will work well for my ESL students.

nodes.label: Frustration with AI's inability to maintain complexity over multiple turns
nodes.grounding_span: I often get frusterated when making multiple choice tests because the AI reverts to very basic levels of questioning and does not follow prompts over many turns in the conversation

nodes.label: Hopeful anticipation of improved AI reliability
nodes.grounding_span: I look forward to it being more honest, and less likely to make mistakes

nodes.label: Awareness of skill atrophy but not concern
nodes.grounding_span: I do sometimes find that it takes me longer to think through the processes that I now tend to delegate to AI. I will definitely get rusty at those things, although I dont think I will lose the ability permanently.

nodes.label: Iterative refinement through multiple prompts and examples
nodes.grounding_span: I have to give the AI multiple prompts and examples and collaborate heavily in order to get the outcome I want.

nodes.label: Calibrated trust based on task type and AI performance history
nodes.grounding_span: At this point I have a pretty good sense for when AI will get things right, and not need as much hand holding, and when AI will make mistakes and I will need to look over that work more to double check.
